meta.title: FOMC Federal Funds Rate at End of 2026
meta.question: What will be the FOMC target federal funds rate upper bound at the conclusion of the December 16-17 2026 FOMC meeting?

priorJustifications.H1: Hold at current 3.50-3.75% is modal — sticky inflation (CPI 3.3% YoY March 2026), Fed on-hold posture, no clear labor weakening signal.
priorJustifications.H2: One cut (3.25-3.50%) is plausible if labor weakens through summer; market pricing is consistent with one cut by year-end.
priorJustifications.H3: Two or more cuts (≤3.25%) requires recession or significant labor shock; tail given current data.
priorJustifications.H4: Historical base rate for 'Fed pivots from cutting bias to hike within 8 months when CPI >3%' is 3-7%. Modest CPI-stickiness premium puts H4 at the upper end of that band, hence 0.05.

rev4_governance_notes.resolution_rule_note: FOMC cut/hike events were REMOVED from the indicator set in REV4 because they are partial resolution events, not Bayesian evidence. Resolution-class observables (any FOMC 25bp+ cut, second cut in 2026, any 25bp+ hike) must be processed via update_posteriors directly with the topic's resolution machinery, NOT via bayesian_update with LRs. Indicator set retained only forward-looking signals.

redTeamGapsAddressed: Current labor market state (U-3, NFP, JOLTS) needed to anchor H3 base rate
Current core PCE level + 3m/6m trajectory needed to anchor H1/H2 split
Powell succession / May 2026 Chair transition is structural regime-change risk for all hypotheses
Treasury issuance + 10y term premium + financial conditions index — non-Fed channel that forces Fed's hand
SEP dot historical accuracy — Goodhart check on Source 1's predictive weight
meta.priorRevisionRationale: Source 3 (Warsh succession) shifts H4 down (hike unlikely under Trump-aligned chair) and H2/H3 up (Trump pressure for cuts). Source 2 (current core PCE 3.2%) still constrains aggressive cutting in near term — moderating the H2/H3 increase. Source 1 (SEP median = 1 cut, range widened) supports H2 as modal.
meta.lensSetReason: Initial lens set during topic creation per skills/news-scan.md mapping

issues: Unfalsifiable hypotheses: H1, H2, H3, H4

blockers: NON-UNIFORM PRIORS without justification — posteriors are {'H1': 0.45, 'H2': 0.35, 'H3': 0.17, 'H4': 0.03} but posteriorHistory has no initial note explaining the asymmetry. Add a posteriorHistory entry documenting why.
warnings: meta.resolution may not be objectively observable — does it describe something a third party could verify?
No anti-indicators defined — every hypothesis needs at least one indicator that would REDUCE its probability

_design_rules: Each indicator must be OBSERVABLE — actions, not opinions
Each must have a clear fired/not-fired threshold
Every hypothesis needs at least one pro AND one anti indicator
Governor flags unfalsifiable hypotheses as DEGRADED

tier1_critical.desc: Chair Powell uses on-record language committing to imminent cut at next meeting ('time has come' / 'appropriate to begin lowering') at any FOMC presser, speech, or congressional testimony. Source: federalreserve.gov.
Chair Powell uses explicit hold-language ('not in a hurry' / 'patient' / 'wait and see') at any FOMC presser, speech, or congressional testimony. Source: federalreserve.gov.
Chair Powell uses hawkish language explicitly leaving hike on the table ('more work to do' / 'risks tilted to upside') at any presser/speech. Source: federalreserve.gov.

tier2_strong.desc: U-3 unemployment >=4.7% in any single monthly BLS print. Source: BLS.
NFP <75k for two consecutive monthly prints. Source: BLS Employment Situation.
Core PCE inflation prints >=3.0% YoY for 2 consecutive monthly releases. Source: BEA.
Core PCE inflation prints <=2.5% YoY for 2 consecutive monthly releases. Source: BEA.
Core PCE prints in 2.5%-3.0% band for 3 consecutive monthly releases (sticky inflation, no clear cut signal). Source: BEA.
U-3 stays in 4.3%-4.6% band for 4 consecutive monthly prints (labor stable, no cut trigger). Source: BLS.
Core PCE prints >=3.5% YoY for any 2 consecutive monthly releases (re-acceleration scenario forces hike consideration). Source: BEA.

tier3_suggestive.desc: Any FOMC voting member uses on-record language explicitly raising hike-back-on-the-table possibility OR FOMC SEP median dot for end-2026 revised >=25bp HIGHER. Source: federalreserve.gov speeches + SEP.
FOMC SEP (Mar/Jun/Sep/Dec) median dot for end-2026 revised >=25bp lower vs prior SEP. Source: FOMC SEP.
Two consecutive FOMC SEPs leave end-2026 median dot unchanged within +/-12.5bp (no rate-path drift). Source: FOMC SEP.

CME FedWatch market-implied probability of >=2 cuts by Dec 2026 exceeds 50% sustained 10 trading days WHILE most-recent SEP shows <=1 cut (market diverges from Fed signal — disagreement signal, not confirmation). Source: CME FedWatch + SEP.

actorModel.description: FOMC December 2026 target rate emerges from interaction of: labor market state, core PCE trajectory, financial conditions, and Powell-led consensus building across hawks/doves. Tariff inflation pulse + labor cooling create unusual two-way risk.

powell.name: Jerome Powell (Chair)
powell.role: Sets agenda framing; controls press conference; swing vote in close decisions.
powell.decisionStyle: Institutionalist, data-dependent, consensus-builder, reluctant to surprise markets.
biases: Anchored to Fed credibility post-2021-23 inflation episode
Cautious on cuts that might require reversal
filters: NFP, U-3, core PCE, financial conditions
overrides: Systemic banking event forces emergency cut regardless of inflation

fomc_hawks.name: FOMC Hawks (Bowman, Waller, regional presidents)
fomc_hawks.role: Constrain Powell's ability to cut without consensus. Influence dot-plot distribution.
fomc_hawks.decisionStyle: Rules-based, prefer systematic frameworks, resistant to cutting before inflation clearly at 2%.
filters: Core PCE persistence, services inflation, wage growth

fomc_doves.name: FOMC Doves (Cook, Jefferson, regional presidents)
fomc_doves.role: Provide vote cushion for cuts when inflation cooperates. Push earlier action on labor side.
fomc_doves.decisionStyle: Balance-of-risks, weight employment leg more heavily.

trump_treasury.name: Trump Administration + Treasury (Bessent)
trump_treasury.role: Tariff policy creates inflation pulse. Public pressure on Fed for cuts. Doesn't set rates but conditions environment.
trump_treasury.decisionStyle: Willing to publicly criticize Fed; transactional on tariff scope.
biases: Tariff revenue framing ignores price effects
filters: Equity markets, dollar level, trade balance
overrides: Visible recession + electoral feedback forces tariff de-escalation

fed_funds_futures.name: CME Fed Funds Futures Market
fed_funds_futures.role: Aggregates private sector expectations. Fed monitors for communication calibration. Persistent over-pricing of cuts is documented bias.
fed_funds_futures.decisionStyle: Bayesian aggregation, fast-moving on data prints.
filters: BLS prints, FOMC speeches, SEP releases

methodology: ACTIONS OVER RHETORIC — FOMC vote is EVENT; SEP/dots are DATA; speeches are RHETORIC. REV4 collapsed Powell tone into per_event_member ternary so we don't double-count one stance signal.

RESOLUTION ROUTING — FOMC cuts/hikes are partial RESOLUTION events for this topic, not Bayesian evidence. Process via update_posteriors when they occur (see meta.rev4_governance_notes.resolution_rule_note).
SEP-VS-MARKET DIVERGENCE — Treat market pricing as DISAGREEMENT signal when it diverges from latest SEP, not confirmation.
BLS RELEASE COUPLING — t2_unemployment_above_47, t2_payrolls_below_75k, and any anti-fires on the same Friday are the SAME OBSERVATION. REV4 binds via causal_event_id=bls_employment_situation.
SOCIALIZATION DETECTION — Coordinated dovish/hawkish messaging across multiple Fed officials in weeks before a meeting = pre-commitment. Higher LR than single-speech signal.

fomc_statements.label: FOMC post-meeting statements + dot-plot + meeting minutes
fomc_statements.note: FOMC cut/hike events are RESOLUTION-class (REV4 — see meta.rev4_governance_notes). Process via update_posteriors not bayesian_update.

fomc_sep.label: FOMC Summary of Economic Projections (dot plot) — 4x/year

powell_speeches.label: Chair Powell speeches + press conferences + Congressional testimony

fed_official_speeches.label: Other FOMC voting member speeches + on-record interviews
fed_official_speeches.note: t3_fed_official_hike_signal — any voting member's hawkish language.

bls_employment_situation.label: BLS Employment Situation (NFP, U-3)
bls_employment_situation.note: Multiple indicators. REV4 binds via causal_event_id=bls_employment_situation.

bea_pce.label: BEA Personal Income & Outlays (core PCE YoY)
bea_pce.note: Drives core_pce indicators across all bands and re-acceleration anti.

cme_fedwatch.label: CME FedWatch market-implied Fed funds path
cme_fedwatch.note: REV4 recast as DIVERGENCE signal (t3_market_pricing_diverges_dovish) when market disagrees with SEP.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF no cuts (3.50-3.75%), THEN 2Y Treasury remains ≥3.5% through year-end
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: CME FedWatch 2Y close ≥3.5% on 2026-12-31

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Assumes yield curve anchoring persists under sustained real-rate stress. AMBER sees regime risk in term-premium dynamics once the pause extends past historical norms.
2Y yield level is a pure aggregate market outcome; no institutional resolution mechanism at stake.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

2Y >=3.5% is directionally fine under a pause scenario, but RED notes that if inflation reaccelerates or UST auction demand cracks, the 2Y could blow through 4.0%+. The ceiling-less phrasing misses the tail where rates go up, not just stay put.
Clean falsifiable CME close metric; directly tests no-cut transmission to 2Y.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF no cuts, THEN Fed publishes Dec FOMC minutes showing no consensus on 2027 rate path with hawkish dissent citing persistent inflation
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Dec 2026 FOMC minutes contain explicit hawkish hold or deferred-2027-cuts language

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Explicit hawkish-dissent framing captures an institutional fracture signal inside the FOMC consensus.
FOMC minutes language is exactly the institutional resolution channel BLUE privileges: Fed communication as an absorptive mechanism managing expectations.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
Names institutional brittleness well: hawkish dissent and no-consensus language is exactly the 'committee fracture' pattern RED looks for. Falsifiable and sharp.
FOMC minutes parsing of 'hawkish language' is subjective; tighten to dissent-vote count.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF no cuts, THEN Dec 2026 FOMC minutes show at least 2 dissenting votes favoring either cut or hike.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H1 holds, THEN core PCE inflation in Dec 2026 remains ≥2.6%
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: BEA Dec 2026 PCE core ≥2.6% YoY

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Inflation persistence at sticky levels is a smooth-trend claim with no imminent regime discontinuity.
Core PCE level is a pure aggregate macro outcome; no explicit resolution-mechanism framing.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Sticky core PCE >=2.6% correctly names the failure mode for the Fed (inflation won't fall to target). This is RED-coherent.
Core PCE >=2.6% is effectively the causal precondition for 'no cuts'; tautological with H1.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut by Sept or Dec, THEN Fed funds futures for Q1 2027 show ≥50% probability of second cut by Nov 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: CME FedWatch probability for Jan or March 2027 cut ≥50% as of 2026-11-30

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Market-implied probability levels are meta-indicators that evolve continuously; no structural threshold.
Fed funds futures pricing a follow-on cut is exactly the market-clearing/forward guidance mechanism BLUE expects: smooth institutional transmission.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
Futures pricing-in further cuts after one cut is consistent with a fragile-Fed narrative. Acceptable.
CME FedWatch probability threshold is a clean instrument.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut, THEN unemployment rises to 4.5-5.0% by Dec 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: BLS Nov 2026 unemployment 4.5-5.0%

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Directionally right that unemployment rises, but a 4.5-5.0% band smooths over the Sahm-threshold crossing that would itself force deeper cuts.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF one cut by Sept, THEN unemployment crosses the Sahm-rule threshold (3m avg >0.5pp above 12m trough) by Dec 2026, triggering market repricing to >=3 cuts in first half 2027.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: A rise to 4.5-5.0% after only one cut overstates labor-market breakdown; Fed guidance + absorption usually prevents a 100bp jump in 8 months from a single-cut regime.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Unemployment rising to 4.5-5.0% with a cut correctly identifies that the cut would be reactive to labor weakness. RED-coherent.
Counterintuitive one-cut-with-rising-unemployment is informative if it resolves.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF no cuts (3.50-3.75%), THEN credit spreads (HY-OAS) widen to 400-450bps by Sept 2026, reflecting accumulated stress from sustained real rates above neutral
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Bloomberg HY OAS index closes 400-450bps range on any date in Sept 2026

criticVerdicts.reasoning: HY-OAS 400-450bps widening captures accumulated-stress threshold crossing, a classic credit-regime break.

HY OAS widening to 400-450bps on sustained pause overstates credit-market breakdown; primary-market access and Fed liquidity facilities typically absorb stress before spreads move this wide without recession.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
HY OAS widening to 400-450bps under sustained restrictive rates is exactly RED's thesis â€” credit is the crack point. Strong.
HY OAS widening under sustained rates is a real stress indicator distinct from GREEN's bullish reads.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF pause continues, THEN 10Y Treasury yield falls to 3.8-4.0% by end-Q3 2026, as global rate cuts (ECB, BoE) and slower US growth drive term-premium compression regardless of Fed action
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: CME 10Y Treasury futures settle in 3.8-4.0% yield range on 2026-09-30

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Term-premium compression through global decoupling is a regime-shift argument, not smooth extrapolation.
Global rate-cut synchronization compressing term premium is a classic institutional-channel absorption story.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
10Y compressing to 3.8-4.0% on global rate cuts is plausible but understates term-premium blowout risk (fiscal, auction failure). Suggest widening band to include an upside tail.
Assumes global rate-cut synchronization; rephrase so term-premium mechanism is falsifiable without that shared assumption.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF no cuts, THEN 10Y term premium (ACM model) declines by at least 30bps between Apr-Sep 2026 regardless of ECB/BoE path.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF pause continues through Q3, THEN residential investment (% of GDP) contracts to 5.8-5.9% by Q3 2026, reflecting housing demand destruction from mortgage rates stuck above 6.5%
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: BEA Q3 2026 GDP release shows residential fixed investment < 6.0% of GDP

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Residential-investment contraction below 6% of GDP is a housing-regime threshold.
Residential investment share is an aggregate; not a specific mechanism claim.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Residential investment contraction to <6% of GDP from mortgage-rate pressure names the housing channel cracking. RED-aligned.
Residential investment pct GDP is sharp and genuinely tests housing demand destruction.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut occurs, THEN 10Y Treasury yield compresses to 3.6-3.8% by end-2026, as global rate-cut cycle synchronizes and reshapes term structure independent of Fed
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: CME 10Y Treasury futures yield 3.6-3.8% on 2026-12-31

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Term-structure reshaping independent of Fed is explicit regime-shift framing.
Global-cycle synchronization reshaping term structure is institutional macro-channel absorption.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
10Y compression to 3.6-3.8% on global cut cycle is coherent with coordinated easing. OK.
Redundant with 208 under different hypothesis; reframe to test rate-cut pass-through specifically.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF one cut, THEN correlation of daily 10Y moves with Fed-funds-futures moves <0.35 in Q4 2026.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut lands in H2 2026, THEN US current account deficit widens to -5.2% to -5.5% of GDP in Q4 2026, reflecting lower real rates boosting demand and import growth
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: BEA Q4 2026 GDP release (Jan 2027) shows current account ≤ -5.2% of GDP

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Current-account widening to -5.2% is a structural macro imbalance signal, correctly framed.
Current-account deficit is an aggregate outcome.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Current account deficit widening to -5.2 to -5.5% under cut cycle correctly flags external imbalance buildup. Strong RED framing.
Current-account-deficit widening is a novel lens not in GREEN portfolio; valuable contrast.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut, THEN corporate debt-to-EBITDA for S&P 500 rises to 2.8-3.0x by Q4 2026, as lower rates enable refinancing and M&A activity accelerates
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Bloomberg or Fed SPF reports median S&P 500 leverage 2.8-3.0x for Q4 2026

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Leverage ratchet under renewed easing captures cycle-regime inflection.
Corporate leverage is an aggregate; mechanism not explicit.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Corporate leverage to 2.8-3.0x on cut-driven refi/M&A is a brittleness accumulation indicator. Good.
Corporate leverage tracking is differentiated and falsifiable.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF no cuts (3.50-3.75%), THEN 2Y Treasury remains ≥3.5% through year-end
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: CME FedWatch 2Y close ≥3.5% on 2026-12-31

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Assumes yield curve anchoring persists under sustained real-rate stress. AMBER sees regime risk in term-premium dynamics once the pause extends past historical norms.
2Y yield level is a pure aggregate market outcome; no institutional resolution mechanism at stake.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.

2Y >=3.5% is directionally fine under a pause scenario, but RED notes that if inflation reaccelerates or UST auction demand cracks, the 2Y could blow through 4.0%+. The ceiling-less phrasing misses the tail where rates go up, not just stay put.
Clean falsifiable CME close metric; directly tests no-cut transmission to 2Y.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF no cuts, THEN Fed publishes Dec FOMC minutes showing no consensus on 2027 rate path with hawkish dissent citing persistent inflation
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: Dec 2026 FOMC minutes contain explicit hawkish hold or deferred-2027-cuts language

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Explicit hawkish-dissent framing captures an institutional fracture signal inside the FOMC consensus.
FOMC minutes language is exactly the institutional resolution channel BLUE privileges: Fed communication as an absorptive mechanism managing expectations.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
Names institutional brittleness well: hawkish dissent and no-consensus language is exactly the 'committee fracture' pattern RED looks for. Falsifiable and sharp.
FOMC minutes parsing of 'hawkish language' is subjective; tighten to dissent-vote count.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF no cuts, THEN Dec 2026 FOMC minutes show at least 2 dissenting votes favoring either cut or hike.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF H1 holds, THEN core PCE inflation in Dec 2026 remains ≥2.6%

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Inflation persistence at sticky levels is a smooth-trend claim with no imminent regime discontinuity.
Core PCE level is a pure aggregate macro outcome; no explicit resolution-mechanism framing.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Sticky core PCE >=2.6% correctly names the failure mode for the Fed (inflation won't fall to target). This is RED-coherent.
Core PCE >=2.6% is effectively the causal precondition for 'no cuts'; tautological with H1.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut by Sept or Dec, THEN Fed funds futures for Q1 2027 show ≥50% probability of second cut by Nov 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: CME FedWatch probability for Jan or March 2027 cut ≥50% as of 2026-11-30

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Market-implied probability levels are meta-indicators that evolve continuously; no structural threshold.
Fed funds futures pricing a follow-on cut is exactly the market-clearing/forward guidance mechanism BLUE expects: smooth institutional transmission.
Names multiple specific decision-makers (agencies, officials, firms, states) and ties the outcome to their choices. Actor-incentive traceability strong.
Futures pricing-in further cuts after one cut is consistent with a fragile-Fed narrative. Acceptable.
CME FedWatch probability threshold is a clean instrument.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF one cut, THEN unemployment rises to 4.5-5.0% by Dec 2026
conditionalPredictions.resolutionCriteria: BLS Nov 2026 unemployment 4.5-5.0%

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Directionally right that unemployment rises, but a 4.5-5.0% band smooths over the Sahm-threshold crossing that would itself force deeper cuts.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF one cut by Sept, THEN unemployment crosses the Sahm-rule threshold (3m avg >0.5pp above 12m trough) by Dec 2026, triggering market repricing to >=3 cuts in first half 2027.

criticVerdicts.reasoning: A rise to 4.5-5.0% after only one cut overstates labor-market breakdown; Fed guidance + absorption usually prevents a 100bp jump in 8 months from a single-cut regime.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
Unemployment rising to 4.5-5.0% with a cut correctly identifies that the cut would be reactive to labor weakness. RED-coherent.
Counterintuitive one-cut-with-rising-unemployment is informative if it resolves.

HY OAS widening to 400-450bps on sustained pause overstates credit-market breakdown; primary-market access and Fed liquidity facilities typically absorb stress before spreads move this wide without recession.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
HY OAS widening to 400-450bps under sustained restrictive rates is exactly RED's thesis â€” credit is the crack point. Strong.
HY OAS widening under sustained rates is a real stress indicator distinct from GREEN's bullish reads.

conditionalPredictions.prediction: IF pause continues, THEN 10Y Treasury yield falls to 3.8-4.0% by end-Q3 2026, as global rate cuts (ECB, BoE) and slower US growth drive term-premium compression regardless of Fed action

criticVerdicts.reasoning: Term-premium compression through global decoupling is a regime-shift argument, not smooth extrapolation.
Global rate-cut synchronization compressing term premium is a classic institutional-channel absorption story.
Pure macro/financial aggregate (index close, yield, inflation print, unemployment, margin). No single named decision-maker controls the reading directly; VIOLET lens neutral.
10Y compressing to 3.8-4.0% on global rate cuts is plausible but understates term-premium blowout risk (fiscal, auction failure). Suggest widening band to include an upside tail.
Assumes global rate-cut synchronization; rephrase so term-premium mechanism is falsifiable without that shared assumption.
criticVerdicts.modified_suggestion: IF no cuts, THEN 10Y term premium (ACM model) declines by at least 30bps between Apr-Sep 2026 regardless of ECB/BoE path.
